ddls:
CREATE TABLE "production_lines" (
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production line */,
  "line_name" TEXT /* Name of the production line */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the production line */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location in the manufacturing facility */,
  "line_type" TEXT /* Type of production line (e.g., assembly, machining, finishing) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status (e.g., active, maintenance, shutdown) */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum production capacity of the line */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "manufacturing_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric record */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line where the metrics were collected */,
  "CT" INTEGER /* Number of cycles */,
  "SZ" INTEGER /* Size of the product */,
  "SH" INTEGER /* Shape of the product */,
  "MA" INTEGER /* Material attributes */,
  "SECS" INTEGER /* Seconds taken for a process */,
  "BN" INTEGER /* Bin number */,
  "BC" INTEGER /* Batch count */,
  "NN" INTEGER /* Nonconformance number */,
  "M" INTEGER /* Metric ID for the material */,
  "CLS" INTEGER /* Class of the product */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the record */,
  "stage" TEXT /* Specific production stage (e.g., machining, assembly, finishing) */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine where the metrics were collected */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator responsible */,
  "material_id" INTEGER /* ID of the material used */,
  "quality_score" INTEGER /* Summarized quality score for the batch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_manufacturing_metrics_material_id FOREIGN KEY ("material_id") REFERENCES material_inventory ("material_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_manufacturing_metrics_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_manufacturing_metrics_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line associated with the maintenance */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the maintenance in hours */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine where the maintenance was performed */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, emergency, preventive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_control" (
  "qc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality control record */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line being inspected */,
  "inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the inspection */,
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the inspector conducting the inspection */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the inspection (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments from the inspector */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch being inspected */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Number of samples inspected */,
  "defect_count" INTEGER /* Number of defective items found */,
  "rework_required" TEXT /* Indicates if rework is required (e.g., yes, no) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("qc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_inspector_id FOREIGN KEY ("inspector_id") REFERENCES inspectors ("inspector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES production_schedule ("batch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inspectors" (
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inspector */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the inspector */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the inspector */,
  "experience_years" INTEGER /* Years of experience in quality control */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Inspector's certification or qualifications */,
  "last_training_date" TEXT /* Date of the last training completed by the inspector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inspector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., performance, quality) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the alert (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for resolving the alert */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Estimated time required to resolve the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, manager, inspector) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "active_status" TEXT /* Active status of the user (e.g., active, disabled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user accessed the system */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., login, view metrics) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert history entry */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert being resolved */,
  "resolved_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolution_comments" TEXT /* Comments regarding the resolution of the alert */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Actual time taken to resolve the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_history_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_history_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_schedule" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production schedule entry */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line for the scheduled run */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being produced */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Scheduled start time of the production run */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Scheduled end time of the production run */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the production run (e.g., planned, in progress, completed) */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch being produced */,
  "expected_output" INTEGER /* Expected number of units to be produced */,
  "actual_output" INTEGER /* Actual number of units produced */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_schedule_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_schedule_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_schedule_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES quality_control ("batch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "standard_quality_score" INTEGER /* Standard quality score for the product */,
  "standard_cycle_time" INTEGER /* Standard cycle time for the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "material_inventory" (
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each material */,
  "material_name" TEXT /* Name of the material */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of material in stock */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement (e.g., kg, liters) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the material in the facility */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier */,
  "last_restock_date" TEXT /* Date of the last restock */,
  "lead_time" INTEGER /* Lead time for restocking the material */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("material_id")
)
